{"key_event":"July Revolution and repressive July Monarchy consolidation","reasoning":"Civil society gained from the 1830 revolution, a stronger bourgeois parliament, municipal reforms, and expanding press/worker mobilization, but censitary exclusion and repression after 1832-35 kept it only moderately strong. The centralized Napoleonic administrative state survived the regime change, suppressed major revolts, restricted the press through the September Laws, and expanded coercive capacity in Algeria, leaving state power slightly stronger than before.","society_change":0.4,"society_power":6.6,"state_change":0.3,"state_power":7.8}
